emerging.label: Action needed
emerging.consequence: There are important gaps in how AI is used in your operational role. Start with data protection and safeguarding awareness — your role puts you in contact with sensitive pupil information.

developing.label: Progress being made
developing.consequence: You have foundational AI awareness in place. Two areas need attention — particularly around data protection and safeguarding — where your role puts you in contact with sensitive pupil information.

established.label: Solid foundations
established.consequence: You show solid operational AI awareness with a few areas to strengthen. Keep focusing on data handling and knowing when to escalate concerns.

strong.label: Strong position
strong.consequence: You demonstrate strong AI readiness for an operations and data-handling role. Sustain your habits as tools and school policies evolve.

leading.label: Leading practice
leading.consequence: You model responsible AI use in a support role — share your approach with colleagues and help build whole-school consistency.

critical.summary: Support staff often work directly with vulnerable pupils or hold sensitive information. AI-specific safeguarding risks — such as deepfakes, impersonation and AI-generated content — may not yet be part of your awareness.
actions: Attend your school's next safeguarding update — check if it covers AI scenarios
Know your DSL's name and when to refer a concern to them
If a pupil mentions something AI-related that worries you, treat it as a safeguarding concern

moderate.summary: Your awareness of AI-specific safeguarding risks could be stronger. Knowing how to recognise and report AI-enabled harm is part of your duty of care.
actions: Ask your DSL whether AI scenarios are covered in your next safeguarding briefing
If something AI-related worries you, report it — do not wait until you are certain

developing.summary: You have some safeguarding awareness — keep building confidence in recognising and escalating AI-related concerns.
actions: Review your school's reporting route for online safety and AI-related incidents
Share any AI concerns with your DSL promptly

critical.summary: Support staff regularly handle pupil records, SEND information and sensitive communications. Entering any of this into a public AI tool — even to help draft a message — is a UK GDPR risk your school is responsible for.
actions: Never enter pupil names, SEND details or sensitive data into a public AI tool
Only use AI tools that appear on your school's approved list
If you're unsure whether something is safe to share with AI, ask your line manager or DPO

moderate.summary: Data protection habits need strengthening. Even small amounts of pupil information in AI tools can create GDPR exposure for your school.
actions: Check your school's approved AI tool list before each new use case
Redact or anonymise information before using AI to help with drafting

developing.summary: Your data protection awareness is developing — tighten habits around what enters AI tools.
actions: Never paste pupil records or SEND information into public AI tools
Ask your DPO or line manager if you are unsure about a specific task

critical.summary: AI-generated emails, letters and reports need careful review before they are sent or acted on.
actions: Always read and edit AI drafts before sending to parents or external contacts
Verify factual claims in AI-generated content against a trusted source

moderate.summary: Build a consistent habit of reviewing AI outputs — especially for communications that leave the school.
actions: Treat every AI draft as a starting point, not a finished document

developing.summary: Keep strengthening your verification habits as AI use grows in your role.
actions: Pause before sending any AI-assisted communication and check names, facts and tone

critical.summary: Use only approved tools and know how to report AI-related data or safeguarding issues.
actions: Check your school's approved AI tool list before using any new tool
Know how to report AI-related data protection or safeguarding concerns

moderate.summary: Strengthen habits around approved tools and escalation routes.
actions: Confirm approval status before using AI for a new task

developing.summary: Keep using approved tools and reporting routes consistently.
actions: Share any unapproved AI tool use with your line manager

critical.summary: Treat AI outputs as drafts that can be wrong — especially for factual or policy information.
actions: Question AI outputs that sound authoritative but may be incorrect
Do not rely on AI for statutory guidance or policy wording without verification

moderate.summary: Build confidence in recognising when AI may be inaccurate or inappropriate for your task.
actions: Cross-check important facts before acting on AI-generated information

developing.summary: Keep building your understanding of what AI can and cannot do reliably.
actions: When AI output surprises you, verify it before sharing or acting on it
